complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Setup Monorepo Structure and Base Configuration
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down monorepo setup into: workspace configuration, package structure creation, TypeScript setup, linting/formatting setup, testing framework setup, and build tooling configuration
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Complex due to multiple tool configurations, package interdependencies, and need for proper module resolution. Requires careful planning of package boundaries and shared configurations.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Define Core Interfaces and Type System
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide into: UIKitAdapter interface design, WillowConfig schema definition, component mapping interfaces, style/token mapping interfaces, AST node type definitions, transformation interfaces, and runtime validation setup
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to need for comprehensive type coverage, forward-thinking interface design, and integration with multiple UI kits. Requires deep understanding of various UI library patterns.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Split into: file loader implementation, configuration merging logic, environment variable support, validation integration, and caching mechanism
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate complexity with well-defined requirements. Main challenges are handling multiple file formats and implementing proper precedence rules.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down into: TypeScript compiler API setup, component detection logic, JSX element parsing, import graph construction, scope analysis implementation, edge case handling, type stripping for JS, and parser optimization
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Very complex due to TypeScript compiler API intricacies, need to handle various component patterns, and complex edge cases like dynamic imports and re-exports.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide into: transformer API design, import transformation logic, component prop mapping, style transformation, namespace/alias handling, rollback mechanism, in-place vs copy transformation, and transformer composition
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Highly complex due to need for reliable AST manipulation, handling various import patterns, and implementing safe rollback mechanisms. Core functionality that must be robust.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Split into: base adapter implementation, Willow reference adapter, Radix UI adapter, Arco Design adapter, Ant Design adapter, component mapping engine, and style system translation
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Complex due to need to understand multiple UI libraries deeply, handle API variations, and create flexible mapping system. Each adapter requires specific domain knowledge.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the remaining code generator tasks into: 1) File writer implementation with directory creation and conflict resolution, 2) Integration testing for template processing with real-world scenarios, 3) Final documentation and template library creation
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: While significant progress has been made (8/12 subtasks complete), the remaining work involves critical file writing functionality and comprehensive integration testing. The reduced subtask count reflects the already completed foundational work.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Implement validation module following SPEC/CREATE/TEST/COMMIT phases: SPEC - Define validation interfaces, engine architecture, and compliance rules (3 tasks). CREATE - Build rules engine, design system validators, and dependency validation (3 tasks). TEST - Unit tests, integration tests, and performance benchmarks (3 tasks). COMMIT - Documentation, templates, and final review (3 tasks)
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is a complex module requiring extensive rule systems, multiple validation types, and comprehensive testing. The 12 subtasks follow the established workflow pattern and ensure thorough coverage of all validation aspects.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Focus on completing the plugin architecture with: 1) Integration testing with sample plugins, 2) Plugin isolation and error boundary testing, 3) Documentation and development guide creation, 4) Starter templates and example plugins
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Core plugin functionality is mostly complete (7/12 subtasks done). The remaining work focuses on testing, documentation, and developer resources. The marketplace preparation has been cancelled, reducing overall complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Build Error Handling and Recovery System
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Build comprehensive error handling following SPEC/CREATE/TEST/COMMIT: SPEC - Define error interfaces, rollback architecture, and recovery patterns (3 tasks). CREATE - Implement error boundaries, rollback mechanisms, and reporting system (3 tasks). TEST - Unit tests, failure scenarios, and rollback reliability (3 tasks). COMMIT - Documentation, cookbook, and operational playbook (3 tasks)
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Error handling is critical infrastructure requiring careful design of boundaries, state management, rollback mechanisms, and recovery strategies. The high complexity demands thorough specification and testing phases.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Develop CLI systematically: SPEC - Define command structure, configuration architecture, and UI patterns (3 tasks). CREATE - Build CLI framework, argument parsing, and interactive components (3 tasks). TEST - Unit tests, integration tests, and performance benchmarks (3 tasks). COMMIT - Documentation, tutorials, and best practices guide (3 tasks)
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: CLI development requires careful attention to user experience, argument handling, configuration management, and interactive components. The structured approach ensures comprehensive coverage of all CLI aspects.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Create testing suite following phases: SPEC - Define strategy, framework architecture, and benchmark metrics (3 tasks). CREATE - Setup unit test framework, integration infrastructure, and benchmarking system (3 tasks). TEST - Write unit tests, integration scenarios, and execute benchmarks (3 tasks). COMMIT - Document best practices, create guides, and setup CI/CD (3 tasks)
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Testing suite requires systematic approach to achieve >90% coverage goal. The phases ensure proper framework setup, comprehensive test coverage, and performance validation with thorough documentation.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Set up comprehensive pre-commit hooks with husky, lint-staged, vitest, ts-prune, and typedoc
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Configure pre-commit hooks systematically: SPEC - Define requirements, execution flow, and configuration patterns (3 tasks). CREATE - Install husky, configure lint-staged, and integrate all tools (3 tasks). TEST - Test violations, benchmark performance, and verify CI compatibility (3 tasks). COMMIT - Document workflow, create troubleshooting guide, and finalize setup (3 tasks)
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Pre-commit hooks involve multiple tool integrations with performance constraints. The structured approach ensures proper configuration, performance optimization, and comprehensive documentation for team adoption.
